channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: K-12 education and the Minnesota Legislature: 5 things to watch this session
item.description: Even as Minnesota lawmakers struggle to start their new session, education committee leaders in the House and Senate are quietly starting to set budget and policy priorities. Here’s a look at what might be coming.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: In a tighter Minnesota Legislature, coalitions pushing middle path could play big role
item.description: The conditions could be ripe for a rise of the Capitol middle, with close margins in both chambers requiring cooperation to move proposals forward.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump fills his government with billionaires after running on a working-class message
item.description: President Donald Trump's second White House is looking a lot like the inside of Mar-a-Lago, with extremely wealthy Americans taking key roles in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump says Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump in his first week back in the White House has offered an early preview to his second-term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US places dozens of senior aid officials on leave, citing possible resistance to Trump orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At least 56 senior officials in the top U.S. aid and development agency were placed on leave Monday amid an investigation into an alleged effort to thwart President Donald Trump’s orders. A current official and a former official at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tucker Carlson's son joins Vance's press office
item.description: Buckley Carlson previously worked as a top aide to Rep. Jim Banks (R-Ind.), the former chairman of the conservative Republican Study Committee and a Trump ally.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Health experts prep Dem lawmakers on anti-vaccine arguments ahead of RFK Jr's confirmation hearings
item.description: Public health experts answered questions from Democratic Senators about whether vaccines cause autism, whether manufacturers should be immune from liability for vaccine injuries, whether vaccines are adequately studied, and more, during a round table discussion about anti-vaccine "misinformatio
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's transportation secretary nominee advances to final Senate vote
item.description: The Senate passed a cloture vote on whether to hold a final confirmation vote for Sean Duffy, Trump's nominee to head the Department of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida legislative leaders respond to DeSantis attacks, imply he didn't read TRUMP Act or is lying
item.description: Florida's congressional leaders fired back at Gov. Ron DeSantis after he accused of them of "theatrics" Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Hegseth at the Helm
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place Mississippi’s voting ban for people convicted of some crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday left in place Mississippi’s Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican state AGs await Trump-brokered TikTok deal, remain skeptical on app safety
item.description: President Donald Trump has signaled a potential deal to save TikTok over the weekend, as Republican attorneys general are skeptical of the app's safety but await Trump's action.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: A CDC official sent a memo to senior leaders at the agency on Sunday night telling them that all agency staff who work with the WHO must immediately stop their collaborations and “await further guidance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department fires more than a dozen key officials on former Special Counsel Jack Smith's team
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: The Justice Department is firing more than a dozen key officials who worked on Special Counsel Jack Smith’s team to prosecute President Trump, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu hopes to meet Trump in Washington as soon as next week, US officials say
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to meet with President Donald Trump in Washington as early as next week.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance political advisers join Ramaswamy ahead of expected Ohio governor run
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy is set to launch his bid mid-February, per the source familiar, to succeed term-limited Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Houston-area school districts address immigration enforcement policies amid federal crackdowns
item.description: In response to President Trump's heightened immigration policies, Houston-area schools and law enforcement are clarifying their protocols regarding federal agency requests, prioritizing student safety.
item.dc:creator: Rilwan Balogun, Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Hegseth, Britt accuse Air Force of 'malicious' pause as it reinstates training on Tuskegee Airmen
item.description: The Air Force has reinstated a course on the first Black pilots unit after it was yanked to comply with President Donald Trump’s executive order banning DEI in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's celebration of American greatness puts a spotlight on a little-known panel of experts
item.description: Within hours of taking office, President Donald Trump outlined in one of his many executive orders a mission to celebrate American greatness and to recognize those who have made contributions throughout history.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers hit a reset after Supreme Court ruling, but stalemate persists
item.description: Like a scene from the movie “Groundhog Day,” life in the Minnesota House continued to repeat itself Monday — a cycle of gaveled to order, no quorum, no business, gaveled to adjournment. The Senate also saw some controversy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Target gets booted from Twin Cities Pride festival after rolling back DEI efforts
item.description: Twin Cities Pride executive director Andi Otto says the nonprofit made the decision because Target is not standing up “against those who are fighting against us.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Churches have a long history of being safe havens
item.description: For centuries, houses of worship have served as havens for people needing refuge — and, in recent decades, sanctuary from the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more refugees to 'just clean out' Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday he'd like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting to "just clean out" the war-torn area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump touts 'no-tax-on-tips' plan in Nevada victory lap
item.description: When Nevada flipped red in November, Trump became the first GOP presidential candidate to win the key swing state since President George W. Bush in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5
